title: OutputFormat.java
description:
This Java file defines the abstract class `OutputFormat` for MapReduce jobs. It specifies output specifications, provides `RecordWriter` for output files, validates output specifications, and manages output committers. The core logic involves abstract methods for `RecordWriter` instantiation, output specification validation, and `OutputCommitter` management. It uses `TaskAttemptContext` and `JobContext` as inputs, and outputs an abstract `RecordWriter` and `OutputCommitter` instances. Dependencies include Hadoop context classes and IO exceptions.

TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Output Directory

DESCRIPTION:
The 'lib/output' directory houses classes like `FileOutputCommitter`, `FileOutputFormat`, and `SequenceFileAsBinaryOutputFormat`. These ensure data integrity, flexibility, and efficiency in job outputs. They handle tasks such as data movement, compression management, unique file naming, and multiple output support. This directory follows a layered architectural pattern, abstracting lower layers to provide a unified interface for diverse output requirements in MapReduce jobs, ensuring robust, adaptable, and efficient output handling.

TITLE: TokenCache for Hadoop MapReduce Security

DESCRIPTION:
The TokenCache.java file manages security tokens, like delegation tokens, between job clients and tasks. It provides methods for token acquisition, cleanup, merging, loading, storage, and key retrieval. This class maintains secure communication within the MapReduce framework, ensuring tasks have necessary credentials for HDFS and cluster interactions. It collaborates with various Hadoop classes and interfaces, and depends on external libraries like SLF4J for logging and Apache Commons Lang for utility functions.

TITLE: Checkpointable Annotation Interface

DESCRIPTION:
The Checkpointable.java file in Hadoop MapReduce defines a marker interface for checkpointable tasks. It uses Java annotations to tag other classes, indicating they can be safely interrupted and restarted. This interface collaborates with Hadoop's task framework, providing metadata without altering task behavior. It depends on Java language classes and Apache Hadoop project packages, processing no input or generating no output, merely annotating other classes for checkpointing.
